headline: Goldman, BofA Delay Fed Cut Calls After ‘Last Straw’ Jobs Data
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Goldman Sachs & Co and Bank of America Corp are the latest in a growing cohort of Wall Street banks pushing back their forecasts for interest-rate cuts, arguing that both jobs and inflation data make a case for the Federal Reserve to keep rates on hold until at least the end of the year.Most Read from BloombergIran Makes New Offer on Uranium in Response to US, WSJ SaysInside a Year of Chaos and Conflict at Kevin Hart’s Media CompanyTrump Rejects New Iran Peace Offer as ‘Totally Un

headline: Affirm price target boosted by Bank of America after strong quarter

headline: How The Bank Of America (BAC) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analyst Views Rebalance
summary: Bank of America's fair value estimate has shifted to US$62.98 from US$62.72, a small price target adjustment that reflects fine tuning rather than a wholesale change in view. This move sits against a backdrop of mixed Street commentary, with some analysts lifting targets by US$1 to US$6 while others are trimming by as much as US$10 and even removing the stock from a US Conviction List. Read on to see what is driving these cross currents and how you can follow the evolving narrative around the...

headline: BofA Just Picked FedEx Stock as One of Its Best Ideas: Logistics Giant Joins the US 1 List
summary: Bank of America has added FedEx (NYSE:FDX) to its “US 1 List,” a collection of its best investment ideas. The move, announced May 11, is a high-conviction symbolic signal even without a fresh price target attached. For long-term investors, the inclusion reframes FedEx stock as one of Wall Street’s preferred industrial transformation plays heading into ... headline: BofA Hikes Affirm Price Target to $88: Clean Beat and Raise Sets Up the May 12 Investor Day
summary: Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) analyst Matthew O’Neill raised his price target on Affirm to $88 from $82 on May 11, while reiterating a Buy rating. The analyst upgrade follows a “clean beat and raise” fiscal Q3 2026 report and frames the recent share dip as “a brief air pocket” ahead of the company’s investor forum. ... headline: Wall Street pushes back Fed rate-cut expectations. Here is why
summary: Investing.com -- Two Wall Street banks have revised their Federal Reserve forecasts, pushing back expected rate cuts as sticky inflation and a resilient labor market reduce the urgency for easing, even as the outlook remains clouded by multiple supply shocks.
